argument_substitution.mechanism: positional expansion: $1, $2 (specific positions), $@ or $ARGUMENTS (all args joined), ${@:N} (args from position N), ${@:N:L} (L args starting at N); richer syntax than most providers

builtin_commands.mechanism: pi prompt templates are entirely user-defined; no built-in slash commands documented

providerExtensions.name: Positional argument expansion in templates
providerExtensions.description: Prompt templates support positional argument substitution: $1, $2, ... for specific positions; $@ or $ARGUMENTS for all arguments joined; ${@:N} for args from position N (1-indexed); ${@:N:L} for L args starting at N. Arguments are passed after the command name (e.g., /component Button 'click handler'). This enables parameterized prompts that expand with user-supplied values.

providerExtensions.name: Optional description frontmatter for autocomplete
providerExtensions.description: Templates accept an optional YAML frontmatter block with a description field. The description is shown in autocomplete when the user types /. If the description is omitted, the first non-empty line of the template body is used as the description. The filename (without .md) becomes the command name.

providerExtensions.name: Multi-source template discovery matching skills pattern
providerExtensions.description: Pi discovers prompt templates from: global ~/.pi/agent/prompts/*.md; project-local .pi/prompts/*.md; package.json pi.prompts entries or prompts/ directories in npm packages; settings.json prompts array (files or directories); --prompt-template CLI flag (repeatable). Discovery in prompts/ directories is non-recursive — subdirectories must be added explicitly. --no-prompt-templates disables all default discovery.
